title: Caloncoba mannii
taxonomy: Caloncoba mannii is a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae. It belongs to the genus Caloncoba, which consists of several species of evergreen trees native to tropical Africa. Caloncoba mannii is a medium-sized tree that can grow up to 15 meters in height, with a straight trunk and smooth bark. The leaves of the Caloncoba mannii are elliptical in shape and have a leathery texture, while the flowers are small and greenish-yellow in color. The plant is known to grow in a variety of habitats, including forests and woodlands, and is often found near water sources.
uses: Caloncoba mannii is reported to have various medicinal uses in traditional medicine. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. In some parts of Africa, Caloncoba mannii is used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential applications.

intro: Caloncoba mannii is a tropical plant species native to West Africa, belonging to the family Ochnaceae and commonly known as African dogwood or African caloncoba. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, and its extracts are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, malaria, and respiratory infections, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antimalarial activities. The bioactive constituents of Caloncoba mannii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in herbal remedies and supplements to treat various health issues.
